diagrams.title: Three-Level DBMS Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the three levels of database architecture: External, Conceptual, and Internal, with arrows indicating schema mappings between levels
diagrams.purpose: Understanding data independence and how users interact with database at different levels

diagrams.title: DBMS Components
diagrams.description: Shows the five main components of a DBMS and their relationships
diagrams.purpose: Understanding what makes up a complete database management system

diagrams.title: File Processing vs DBMS
diagrams.description: Comparison diagram showing disadvantages of file processing and advantages of DBMS approach
elements: File Processing: Separate files, Data redundancy, No security, Data inconsistency
DBMS: Centralized, Controlled redundancy, Security, Consistency
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why DBMS is preferred over traditional file processing systems

diagrams.title: DBMS Language Categories
diagrams.description: Shows different types of DBMS languages and their commands
diagrams.purpose: Understanding different database languages and their purposes
